revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:祇園神社]] to [[Category:Gion shrines]][[Category:Mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=虎柏神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[拝殿]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[東京都]][[青梅市]][[根ヶ布]]1-316 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[大歳御祖神]]
[[オモダル・アヤカシコネ|惶根神]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=[[式内社]]（[[論社]]）（小社）・旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[三間社]][[切妻造]]{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}}
（覆殿の中にあり見ることはできない） | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival=[[8月26日]]～[[8月28日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=虎柏神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''虎柏神社'''（とらかしわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[青梅市]]根ヶ布にある[[神社]]である。旧称、[[諏訪神社]]。古社で、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]八座{{efn|[[阿伎留神社]]（[[あきる野市]]）・小野神社（[[多摩市]][[小野神社 (多摩市)|小野神社]]と[[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[小野神社 (東京都府中市)|小野神社]]が[[論社]]）・[[布多天神社]]（[[調布市]]）・[[武蔵御嶽神社|御嶽神社]]（青梅市）・[[阿豆佐味天神社]]（瑞穂町）・[[穴澤天神社]]（[[稲城市]]）・'''虎柏神社'''（青梅市）・[[青渭神社]]（調布市・青梅市・稲城市）}}の一座に数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[正殿]]（虎柏神社） ** [[大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ） ** [[オモダル・アヤカシコネ|惶根神]]（かしこねのかみ） * 東[[相殿]]（[[諏訪神社|諏訪上下神社]]） ** [[建御名方命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） ** [[八坂刀売命]]（やさかとめのみこと） * 西相殿（[[八雲神社]]） ** [[素盞嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） ** [[事代主命]]（ことしろぬしのみこと） == 歴史 == [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[鳥居]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[銘板]]]] 創建時期は不詳であるが[[崇神天皇]]の御代に[[神戸 (民戸)|神戸]]を寄進されたと伝えられ、いわゆる[[延喜式内社]]に比定される古社である。 [[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）に六孫王・[[源経基]]が[[諏訪大社]]より諏訪上下神を勧請し、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）[[勝沼城]]主、[[三田氏宗]]により再興されたと伝えられ、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の「[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]小曽木郷惣社[[縁起]]」には、[[天正]]16年（[[1588年]]）[[浅野長政]]が[[除疫神]]（[[牛頭天王]]）を[[勧請]]したと伝えられている。これが[[八雲神社]]である。[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）には[[正殿]]に諏訪上下神、東の[[相殿]]に虎柏神、西の相殿に除疫神を定め、小曽木郷の[[総社]]を号したと記されており、[[江戸時代]]は諏訪明神、諏訪宮と称し、今でもお諏訪様と通称される。最寄り[[バス停]]の名称は現在でも「諏訪神社」である。 天正19年（[[1591年]]）には[[徳川氏]]より、[[朱印地]]三[[石 (単位)|石]]を下賜され、朱印高は[[幕末]]まで変わることはなかった。[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、それまで脇殿に祀られていた虎柏神を正殿に遷し、諏訪上下神を東の相殿として、虎柏神社の旧称に復した。明治6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列する。 現在の本殿は[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）の再建で、[[三間社]][[切妻造]]だが、[[覆殿]]の中にあって外から見ることはできない。[[末社]]の高峯神社と[[稲荷神社]]は諏訪神と同じく源経基が勧請したものであり、稲荷神社は、もとは西の相殿に祀られていたという。本殿や江戸時代以降に寄進された石造物及び古木の[[鎮守の森|境内林]]などは、江戸・明治期の旧態を留めて宗教的神秘性を有する独特な空間を形成している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に虎柏神社があり、[[論社]]に[[調布市]]の虎狛（こはく）神社があるが、これは狛の字を柏に写し間違えたのだとも言われている。虎柏神社では銘板を設置するなどして、式内社であることの宣揚に努めている。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Temizusha.jpg|手水舎 == 例大祭 == *[[8月26日]]（御殿（おとの）入り祭） *[[8月27日]]（[[椀飯]]の式・高峯神社祭） *[[8月28日]]（お炊き上げ・[[例大祭]]・[[奉納]][[相撲]]） == 摂社・末社 == '''相殿''' * 八雲神社 * 諏訪神社 '''末社''' * [[藤原神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。御祭神は倉稲魂命。 * [[稲荷神社]] : 天正年間（1573年～1593年）に藤原右衛門太夫が勧請した。勧請した時は西の相殿に祀られていたが、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は天児屋根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Hujiwara-jinja.jpg|藤原神社 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Asahinokariya.jpg|朝日の仮屋 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社と夕日の仮屋 '''境外社''' * [[高峯神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。江戸時代は虎柏神社の相殿に祀られていた{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 根布村}}が、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は味耜高彦根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Kyu-sandou.jpg|高峯神社の旧参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Torii.jpg|高峯神社の鳥居 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Sandou.jpg|高峯神社の現在の参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja.jpg|高峯神社の石祠 == 文化財 == * 都指定[[有形文化財]]（建造物） ** [[本殿]]（附・[[棟札]]1枚）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}} * 都指定[[史跡]] ** 虎柏神社境域（附・虎柏神社境域関係資料2点）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社境域}} * 都指定[[無形民俗文化財]]（風俗慣習） ** 虎柏神社の[[祭礼]]行事（附・行事関係記録231点）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社の祭礼行事}} == 交通 == * [[青梅線]]、[[東青梅駅]]から徒歩20分 * 同駅から成木方面行き[[都営バス]]、「諏訪神社」下車 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ118多磨郡ノ30|chapter=根布村 諏訪明神社|id={{NDLJP|763994/37}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 根布村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=193|title=虎柏神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|accessdate=2020-7-22|ref={{sfnref|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=565|title=虎柏神社境域|website=東京都文化財情報データベース |accessdate=2020-7-22|ref={{sfnref|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社境域}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url= https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=461|title=青梅市虎柏神社の祭礼行事 附祭礼行事関係記録 231点|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|accessdate=2020-7-22|ref={{sfnref|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社の祭礼行事}}}} == 関連項目 [[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）に六孫王・[[源経基]]が[[諏訪大社]]より諏訪上下神を勧請し、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）[[勝沼城]]主、[[三田氏宗]]により再興されたと伝えられ、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の「[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]小曽木郷惣社[[縁起]]」には、[[天正]]16年（[[1588年]]）[[浅野長政]]が[[除疫神]]（[[牛頭天王]]）を[[勧請]]したと伝えられている。これが[[八雲神社]]である。[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）には[[正殿]]に諏訪上下神、東の[[相殿]]に虎柏神、西の相殿に除疫神を定め、小曽木郷の[[総社]]を号したと記されており、[[江戸時代]]は諏訪明神、諏訪宮と称し、今でもお諏訪様と通称される。最寄り[[バス停]]の名称は現在でも「諏訪神社」である。 天正19年（[[1591年]]）には[[徳川氏]]より、[[朱印地]]三[[石 (単位)|石]]を下賜され、朱印高は[[幕末]]まで変わることはなかった。[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、それまで脇殿に祀られていた虎柏神を正殿に遷し、諏訪上下神を東の相殿として、虎柏神社の旧称に復した。明治6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列する。 現在の本殿は[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）の再建で、[[三間社]][[切妻造]]だが、[[覆殿]]の中にあって外から見ることはできない。[[末社]]の高峯神社と[[稲荷神社]]は諏訪神と同じく源経基が勧請したものであり、稲荷神社は、もとは西の相殿に祀られていたという。本殿や江戸時代以降に寄進された石造物及び古木の[[鎮守の森|境内林]]などは、江戸・明治期の旧態を留めて宗教的神秘性を有する独特な空間を形成している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に虎柏神社があり、[[論社]]に[[調布市]]の虎狛（こはく）神社があるが、これは狛の字を柏に写し間違えたのだとも言われている。虎柏神社では銘板を設置するなどして、式内社であることの宣揚に努めている。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Temizusha.jpg|手水舎 == 例大祭 == *[[8月26日]]（御殿（おとの）入り祭） *[[8月27日]]（[[椀飯]]の式・高峯神社祭） *[[8月28日]]（お炊き上げ・[[例大祭]]・[[奉納]][[相撲]]） == 摂社・末社 == '''相殿''' * 八雲神社 * 諏訪神社 '''末社''' * [[藤原神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。御祭神は倉稲魂命。 * [[稲荷神社]] : 天正年間（1573年～1593年）に藤原右衛門太夫が勧請した。勧請した時は西の相殿に祀られていたが、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は天児屋根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Hujiwara-jinja.jpg|藤原神社 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Asahinokariya.jpg|朝日の仮屋 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社と夕日の仮屋 '''境外社''' * [[高峯神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。江戸時代は虎柏神社の相殿に祀られていた{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 根布村}}が、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は味耜高彦根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Kyu-sandou.jpg|高峯神社の旧参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Torii.jpg|高峯神社の鳥居 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Sandou.jpg|高峯神社の現在の参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja.jpg|高峯神社の石祠 == 文化財 == * 都指定[[有形文化財]]（建造物） ** [[本殿]]（附・[[棟札]]1枚）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}} * 都指定[[史跡]] ** 虎柏神社境域（附・虎柏神社境域関係資料2点）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社境域}} * 都指定[[無形民俗文化財]]（風俗慣習） ** 虎柏神社の[[祭礼]]行事（附・行事関係記録231点）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社の祭礼行事}} == 交通 == * [[青梅線]]、[[東青梅駅]]から徒歩20分 * 同駅から成木方面行き[[都営バス]]、「諏訪神社」下車 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ118多磨郡ノ30|chapter=根布村 諏訪明神社|id={{NDLJP|763994/37}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 根布村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=193|title=虎柏神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|accessdate=2020-7-22|ref={{sfnref|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}}}} * {{Cite [[拝殿]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[東京都]][[青梅市]][[根ヶ布]]1-316 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[大歳御祖神]]

（覆殿の中にあり見ることはできない） | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival=[[8月26日]]～[[8月28日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''虎柏神社'''（とらかしわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[青梅市]]根ヶ布にある[[神社]]である。旧称、[[諏訪神社]]。古社で、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]八座{{efn|[[阿伎留神社]]（[[あきる野市]]）・小野神社（[[多摩市]][[小野神社 (多摩市)|小野神社]]と[[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[小野神社 (東京都府中市)|小野神社]]が[[論社]]）・[[布多天神社]]（[[調布市]]）・[[武蔵御嶽神社|御嶽神社]]（青梅市）・[[阿豆佐味天神社]]（瑞穂町）・[[穴澤天神社]]（[[稲城市]]）・'''虎柏神社'''（青梅市）・[[青渭神社]]（調布市・青梅市・稲城市）}}の一座に数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[正殿]]（虎柏神社） ** [[大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ） ** [[オモダル・アヤカシコネ|惶根神]]（かしこねのかみ） * 東[[相殿]]（[[諏訪神社|諏訪上下神社]]） ** [[建御名方命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） ** [[八坂刀売命]]（やさかとめのみこと） * 西相殿（[[八雲神社]]） ** [[素盞嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） ** [[事代主命]]（ことしろぬしのみこと） == 歴史 == [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[鳥居]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[銘板]]]] 創建時期は不詳であるが[[崇神天皇]]の御代に[[神戸 (民戸)|神戸]]を寄進されたと伝えられ、いわゆる[[延喜式内社]]に比定される古社である。 [[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）に六孫王・[[源経基]]が[[諏訪大社]]より諏訪上下神を勧請し、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）[[勝沼城]]主、[[三田氏宗]]により再興されたと伝えられ、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の「[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]小曽木郷惣社[[縁起]]」には、[[天正]]16年（[[1588年]]）[[浅野長政]]が[[除疫神]]（[[牛頭天王]]）を[[勧請]]したと伝えられている。これが[[八雲神社]]である。[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）には[[正殿]]に諏訪上下神、東の[[相殿]]に虎柏神、西の相殿に除疫神を定め、小曽木郷の[[総社]]を号したと記されており、[[江戸時代]]は諏訪明神、諏訪宮と称し、今でもお諏訪様と通称される。最寄り[[バス停]]の名称は現在でも「諏訪神社」である。 天正19年（[[1591年]]）には[[徳川氏]]より、[[朱印地]]三[[石 (単位)|石]]を下賜され、朱印高は[[幕末]]まで変わることはなかった。[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、それまで脇殿に祀られていた虎柏神を正殿に遷し、諏訪上下神を東の相殿として、虎柏神社の旧称に復した。明治6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列する。 現在の本殿は[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）の再建で、[[三間社]][[切妻造]]だが、[[覆殿]]の中にあって外から見ることはできない。[[末社]]の高峯神社と[[稲荷神社]]は諏訪神と同じく源経基が勧請したものであり、稲荷神社は、もとは西の相殿に祀られていたという。本殿や江戸時代以降に寄進された石造物及び古木の[[鎮守の森|境内林]]などは、江戸・明治期の旧態を留めて宗教的神秘性を有する独特な空間を形成している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に虎柏神社があり、[[論社]]に[[調布市]]の虎狛（こはく）神社があるが、これは狛の字を柏に写し間違えたのだとも言われている。虎柏神社では銘板を設置するなどして、式内社であることの宣揚に努めている。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) '''虎柏神社'''（とらかしわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[青梅市]]根ヶ布にある[[神社]]である。旧称、[[諏訪神社]]。古社で、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]八座{{efn|[[阿伎留神社]]（[[あきる野市]]）・小野神社（[[多摩市]][[小野神社 (多摩市)|小野神社]]と[[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[小野神社 (東京都府中市)|小野神社]]が[[論社]]）・[[布多天神社]]（[[調布市]]）・[[武蔵御嶽神社|御嶽神社]]（青梅市）・[[阿豆佐味天神社]]（瑞穂町）・[[穴澤天神社]]（[[稲城市]]）・'''虎柏神社'''（青梅市）・[[青渭神社]]（調布市・青梅市・稲城市）}}の一座に数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[正殿]]（虎柏神社） ** [[大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ） ** [[オモダル・アヤカシコネ|惶根神]]（かしこねのかみ） * 東[[相殿]]（[[諏訪神社|諏訪上下神社]]） ** [[建御名方命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） ** [[八坂刀売命]]（やさかとめのみこと） * 西相殿（[[八雲神社]]） ** [[素盞嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） ** [[事代主命]]（ことしろぬしのみこと） == 歴史 == [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[鳥居]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[銘板]]]] 創建時期は不詳であるが[[崇神天皇]]の御代に[[神戸 (民戸)|神戸]]を寄進されたと伝えられ、いわゆる[[延喜式内社]]に比定される古社である。 [[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）に六孫王・[[源経基]]が[[諏訪大社]]より諏訪上下神を勧請し、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）[[勝沼城]]主、[[三田氏宗]]により再興されたと伝えられ、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の「[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]小曽木郷惣社[[縁起]]」には、[[天正]]16年（[[1588年]]）[[浅野長政]]が[[除疫神]]（[[牛頭天王]]）を[[勧請]]したと伝えられている。これが[[八雲神社]]である。[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）には[[正殿]]に諏訪上下神、東の[[相殿]]に虎柏神、西の相殿に除疫神を定め、小曽木郷の[[総社]]を号したと記されており、[[江戸時代]]は諏訪明神、諏訪宮と称し、今でもお諏訪様と通称される。最寄り[[バス停]]の名称は現在でも「諏訪神社」である。 天正19年（[[1591年]]）には[[徳川氏]]より、[[朱印地]]三[[石 (単位)|石]]を下賜され、朱印高は[[幕末]]まで変わることはなかった。[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、それまで脇殿に祀られていた虎柏神を正殿に遷し、諏訪上下神を東の相殿として、虎柏神社の旧称に復した。明治6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列する。 現在の本殿は[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）の再建で、[[三間社]][[切妻造]]だが、[[覆殿]]の中にあって外から見ることはできない。[[末社]]の高峯神社と[[稲荷神社]]は諏訪神と同じく源経基が勧請したものであり、稲荷神社は、もとは西の相殿に祀られていたという。本殿や江戸時代以降に寄進された石造物及び古木の[[鎮守の森|境内林]]などは、江戸・明治期の旧態を留めて宗教的神秘性を有する独特な空間を形成している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に虎柏神社があり、[[論社]]に[[調布市]]の虎狛（こはく）神社があるが、これは狛の字を柏に写し間違えたのだとも言われている。虎柏神社では銘板を設置するなどして、式内社であることの宣揚に努めている。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Temizusha.jpg|手水舎 == 例大祭 == *[[8月26日]]（御殿（おとの）入り祭） *[[8月27日]]（[[椀飯]]の式・高峯神社祭） *[[8月28日]]（お炊き上げ・[[例大祭]]・[[奉納]][[相撲]]） == 摂社・末社 == '''相殿''' * 八雲神社 * 諏訪神社 '''末社''' * [[藤原神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。御祭神は倉稲魂命。 * [[稲荷神社]] : 天正年間（1573年～1593年）に藤原右衛門太夫が勧請した。勧請した時は西の相殿に祀られていたが、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は天児屋根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Hujiwara-jinja.jpg|藤原神社 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Asahinokariya.jpg|朝日の仮屋 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社と夕日の仮屋 '''境外社''' * [[高峯神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。江戸時代は虎柏神社の相殿に祀られていた{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 根布村}}が、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は味耜高彦根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Kyu-sandou.jpg|高峯神社の旧参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Torii.jpg|高峯神社の鳥居 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Sandou.jpg|高峯神社の現在の参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja.jpg|高峯神社の石祠 == 文化財 == * 都指定[[有形文化財]]（建造物） ** [[本殿]]（附・[[棟札]]1枚）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}} * 都指定[[史跡]] ** Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft|draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |draft}} [[ja:虎柏神社]] {{afc comment|1=surprisingly notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 17:54, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|draft}} is a regional [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. ## Enshrined Deities ### Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] ### Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] ### Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] ## History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. ## festivals - **August 26** - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - **August 27** - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - **August 28** - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament ## Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines ### **Enshrined Shrines** - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine ### **Subsidiary Shrines** - **Fujiwara Shrine** - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - **Inari Shrine** - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. ### **Off-site Shrine** - **Takamine Shrine** - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. ## Cultural Properties - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property** (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site** - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property** (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} ## Access - **Rail**: 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - **Bus**: Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop ## References {{Reflist}} ## Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] ## External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. ## Enshrined Deities ### Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] ### Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] ### Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] ## History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. ## festivals - **August 26** - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - **August 27** - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - **August 28** - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament ## Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines ### **Enshrined Shrines** - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine ### **Subsidiary Shrines** - **Fujiwara Shrine** - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - **Inari Shrine** - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. ### **Off-site Shrine** - **Takamine Shrine** - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. ## Cultural Properties - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property** (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site** - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property** (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} ## Access - **Rail**: 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - **Bus**: Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop ## References {{Reflist}} ## Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] ## External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. ## Enshrined Deities ### Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] ### Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] ### Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] ## History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. ## festivals - **August 26** - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - **August 27** - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - **August 28** - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament ## Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines ### **Enshrined Shrines** - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine ### **Subsidiary Shrines** - **Fujiwara Shrine** - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - **Inari Shrine** - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. ### **Off-site Shrine** - **Takamine Shrine** - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. ## Cultural Properties - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property** (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site** - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property** (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} ## Access - **Rail**: 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - **Bus**: Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop ## References {{Reflist}} ## Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] ## External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines {{神社 |名称=虎柏神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]

Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=虎柏神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''虎柏神社'''（とらかしわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[青梅市]]根ヶ布にある[[神社]]である。旧称、[[諏訪神社]]。古社で、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]八座{{efn|[[阿伎留神社]]（[[あきる野市]]）・小野神社（[[多摩市]][[小野神社 (多摩市)|小野神社]]と[[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[小野神社 (東京都府中市)|小野神社]]が[[論社]]）・[[布多天神社]]（[[調布市]]）・[[武蔵御嶽神社|御嶽神社]]（青梅市）・[[阿豆佐味天神社]]（瑞穂町）・[[穴澤天神社]]（[[稲城市]]）・'''虎柏神社'''（青梅市）・[[青渭神社]]（調布市・青梅市・稲城市）}}の一座に数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[正殿]]（虎柏神社） ** [[大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ） ** [[オモダル・アヤカシコネ|惶根神]]（かしこねのかみ） * 東[[相殿]]（[[諏訪神社|諏訪上下神社]]） ** [[建御名方命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） ** [[八坂刀売命]]（やさかとめのみこと） * 西相殿（[[八雲神社]]） ** [[素盞嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） ** [[事代主命]]（ことしろぬしのみこと） == 歴史 == [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[鳥居]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[銘板]]]] 創建時期は不詳であるが[[崇神天皇]]の御代に[[神戸 (民戸)|神戸]]を寄進されたと伝えられ、いわゆる[[延喜式内社]]に比定される古社である。 [[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）に六孫王・[[源経基]]が[[諏訪大社]]より諏訪上下神を勧請し、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）[[勝沼城]]主、[[三田氏宗]]により再興されたと伝えられ、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の「[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]小曽木郷惣社[[縁起]]」には、[[天正]]16年（[[1588年]]）[[浅野長政]]が[[除疫神]]（[[牛頭天王]]）を[[勧請]]したと伝えられている。これが[[八雲神社]]である。[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）には[[正殿]]に諏訪上下神、東の[[相殿]]に虎柏神、西の相殿に除疫神を定め、小曽木郷の[[総社]]を号したと記されており、[[江戸時代]]は諏訪明神、諏訪宮と称し、今でもお諏訪様と通称される。最寄り[[バス停]]の名称は現在でも「諏訪神社」である。 天正19年（[[1591年]]）には[[徳川氏]]より、[[朱印地]]三[[石 (単位)|石]]を下賜され、朱印高は[[幕末]]まで変わることはなかった。[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、それまで脇殿に祀られていた虎柏神を正殿に遷し、諏訪上下神を東の相殿として、虎柏神社の旧称に復した。明治6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列する。 現在の本殿は[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）の再建で、[[三間社]][[切妻造]]だが、[[覆殿]]の中にあって外から見ることはできない。[[末社]]の高峯神社と[[稲荷神社]]は諏訪神と同じく源経基が勧請したものであり、稲荷神社は、もとは西の相殿に祀られていたという。本殿や江戸時代以降に寄進された石造物及び古木の[[鎮守の森|境内林]]などは、江戸・明治期の旧態を留めて宗教的神秘性を有する独特な空間を形成している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に虎柏神社があり、[[論社]]に[[調布市]]の虎狛（こはく）神社があるが、これは狛の字を柏に写し間違えたのだとも言われている。虎柏神社では銘板を設置するなどして、式内社であることの宣揚に努めている。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Temizusha.jpg|手水舎 == 例大祭 == *[[8月26日]]（御殿（おとの）入り祭） *[[8月27日]]（[[椀飯]]の式・高峯神社祭） *[[8月28日]]（お炊き上げ・[[例大祭]]・[[奉納]][[相撲]]） == 摂社・末社 == '''相殿''' * 八雲神社 * 諏訪神社 '''末社''' * [[藤原神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。御祭神は倉稲魂命。 * [[稲荷神社]] : 天正年間（1573年～1593年）に藤原右衛門太夫が勧請した。勧請した時は西の相殿に祀られていたが、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は天児屋根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Hujiwara-jinja.jpg|藤原神社 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Asahinokariya.jpg|朝日の仮屋 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社と夕日の仮屋 '''境外社''' * [[高峯神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。江戸時代は虎柏神社の相殿に祀られていた{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 根布村}}が、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は味耜高彦根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Kyu-sandou.jpg|高峯神社の旧参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Torii.jpg|高峯神社の鳥居 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Sandou.jpg|高峯神社の現在の参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja.jpg|高峯神社の石祠 == 文化財 == * 都指定[[有形文化財]]（建造物） ** [[本殿]]（附・[[棟札]]1枚）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}} * 都指定[[史跡]] ** Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft|draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |draft}} [[ja:虎柏神社]] {{afc comment|1=surprisingly notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 17:54, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|draft}} is a regional [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. ## Enshrined Deities ### Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] ### Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] ### Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] ## History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. ## festivals - **August 26** - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - **August 27** - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - **August 28** - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament ## Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines ### **Enshrined Shrines** - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine ### **Subsidiary Shrines** - **Fujiwara Shrine** - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - **Inari Shrine** - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. ### **Off-site Shrine** - **Takamine Shrine** - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. ## Cultural Properties - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property** (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site** - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property** (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} ## Access - **Rail**: 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - **Bus**: Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop ## References {{Reflist}} ## Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] ## External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines [[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. ## Enshrined Deities === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] ## History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. ## festivals - **August 26** - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - **August 27** - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - **August 28** - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament ## Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines === **Enshrined Shrines** - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === **Subsidiary Shrines** - **Fujiwara Shrine** - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - **Inari Shrine** - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === **Off-site Shrine** - **Takamine Shrine** - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. ## Cultural Properties - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property** (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site** - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property** (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} ## Access - **Rail**: 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - **Bus**: Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop ## References {{Reflist}} ## Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] ## External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines [[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals - **August 26** - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - **August 27** - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - **August 28** - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines === **Enshrined Shrines** - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === **Subsidiary Shrines** - **Fujiwara Shrine** - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - **Inari Shrine** - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === **Off-site Shrine** - **Takamine Shrine** - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property** (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site** - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - **Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property** (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access - **Rail**: 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - **Bus**: Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References {{Reflist}} == Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines [[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals - '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines === '''Enshrined Shrines''' - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines''' - '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine''' - '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access - '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References {{Reflist}} == Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} [[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals - '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines === '''Enshrined Shrines''' - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines''' - '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine''' - '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access - '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References {{Reflist}} == Related Articles - revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=虎柏神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[拝殿]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[東京都]][[青梅市]][[根ヶ布]]1-316 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=[[大歳御祖神]]
[[オモダル・アヤカシコネ|惶根神]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=[[式内社]]（[[論社]]）（小社）・旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[三間社]][[切妻造]]{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}}
（覆殿の中にあり見ることはできない） | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival=[[8月26日]]～[[8月28日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=虎柏神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''虎柏神社'''（とらかしわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[青梅市]]根ヶ布にある[[神社]]である。旧称、[[諏訪神社]]。古社で、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]八座{{efn|[[阿伎留神社]]（[[あきる野市]]）・小野神社（[[多摩市]][[小野神社 (多摩市)|小野神社]]と[[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[小野神社 (東京都府中市)|小野神社]]が[[論社]]）・[[布多天神社]]（[[調布市]]）・[[武蔵御嶽神社|御嶽神社]]（青梅市）・[[阿豆佐味天神社]]（瑞穂町）・[[穴澤天神社]]（[[稲城市]]）・'''虎柏神社'''（青梅市）・[[青渭神社]]（調布市・青梅市・稲城市）}}の一座に数えられている。 == 祭神 == * [[正殿]]（虎柏神社） ** [[大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ） ** [[オモダル・アヤカシコネ|惶根神]]（かしこねのかみ） * 東[[相殿]]（[[諏訪神社|諏訪上下神社]]） ** [[建御名方命]]（たけみなかたのみこと） ** [[八坂刀売命]]（やさかとめのみこと） * 西相殿（[[八雲神社]]） ** [[素盞嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） ** [[事代主命]]（ことしろぬしのみこと） == History == [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[鳥居]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[銘板]]]] 創建時期は不詳であるが[[崇神天皇]]の御代に[[神戸 (民戸)|神戸]]を寄進されたと伝えられ、いわゆる[[延喜式内社]]に比定される古社である。 [[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）に六孫王・[[源経基]]が[[諏訪大社]]より諏訪上下神を勧請し、[[永正]]年間（[[1504年]] - [[1521年]]）[[勝沼城]]主、[[三田氏宗]]により再興されたと伝えられ、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の「[[武蔵国]][[多摩郡]]小曽木郷惣社[[縁起]]」には、[[天正]]16年（[[1588年]]）[[浅野長政]]が[[除疫神]]（[[牛頭天王]]）を[[勧請]]したと伝えられている。これが[[八雲神社]]である。[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）には[[正殿]]に諏訪上下神、東の[[相殿]]に虎柏神、西の相殿に除疫神を定め、小曽木郷の[[総社]]を号したと記されており、[[江戸時代]]は諏訪明神、諏訪宮と称し、今でもお諏訪様と通称される。最寄り[[バス停]]の名称は現在でも「諏訪神社」である。 天正19年（[[1591年]]）には[[徳川氏]]より、[[朱印地]]三[[石 (単位)|石]]を下賜され、朱印高は[[幕末]]まで変わることはなかった。[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、それまで脇殿に祀られていた虎柏神を正殿に遷し、諏訪上下神を東の相殿として、虎柏神社の旧称に復した。明治6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列する。 現在の本殿は[[享保]]19年（[[1734年]]）の再建で、[[三間社]][[切妻造]]だが、[[覆殿]]の中にあって外から見ることはできない。[[末社]]の高峯神社と[[稲荷神社]]は諏訪神と同じく源経基が勧請したものであり、稲荷神社は、もとは西の相殿に祀られていたという。本殿や江戸時代以降に寄進された石造物及び古木の[[鎮守の森|境内林]]などは、江戸・明治期の旧態を留めて宗教的神秘性を有する独特な空間を形成している。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に虎柏神社があり、[[論社]]に[[調布市]]の虎狛（こはく）神社があるが、これは狛の字を柏に写し間違えたのだとも言われている。虎柏神社では銘板を設置するなどして、式内社であることの宣揚に努めている。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Temizusha.jpg|手水舎 == 例大祭 == *[[8月26日]]（御殿（おとの）入り祭） *[[8月27日]]（[[椀飯]]の式・高峯神社祭） *[[8月28日]]（お炊き上げ・[[例大祭]]・[[奉納]][[相撲]]） == 摂社・末社 == '''相殿''' * 八雲神社 * 諏訪神社 '''末社''' * [[藤原神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。御祭神は倉稲魂命。 * [[稲荷神社]] : 天正年間（1573年～1593年）に藤原右衛門太夫が勧請した。勧請した時は西の相殿に祀られていたが、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は天児屋根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Hujiwara-jinja.jpg|藤原神社 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Asahinokariya.jpg|朝日の仮屋 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社と夕日の仮屋 '''境外社''' * [[高峯神社]] : 天慶年間（938年～947年）に源経基が諏訪上下神とと同時に勧請した。江戸時代は虎柏神社の相殿に祀られていた{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 根布村}}が、現在は虎柏神社の社殿の外に祀られている。御祭神は味耜高彦根命。 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Kyu-sandou.jpg|高峯神社の旧参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Torii.jpg|高峯神社の鳥居 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja Sandou.jpg|高峯神社の現在の参道 File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Takamine-jinja.jpg|高峯神社の石祠 == 文化財 == * 都指定[[有形文化財]]（建造物） ** [[本殿]]（附・[[棟札]]1枚）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}} * 都指定[[史跡]] ** 虎柏神社境域（附・虎柏神社境域関係資料2点）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社境域}} * 都指定[[無形民俗文化財]]（風俗慣習） ** 虎柏神社の[[祭礼]]行事（附・行事関係記録231点）{{sfn|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社の祭礼行事}} == 交通 == * [[青梅線]]、[[東青梅駅]]から徒歩20分 * 同駅から成木方面行き[[都営バス]]、「諏訪神社」下車 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ118多磨郡ノ30|chapter=根布村 諏訪明神社|id={{NDLJP|763994/37}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 根布村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=193|title=虎柏神社本殿|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|accessdate=2020-7-22|ref={{sfnref|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社本殿}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=565|title=虎柏神社境域|website=東京都文化財情報データベース |accessdate=2020-7-22|ref={{sfnref|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社境域}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url= https://bunkazai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/jp/search_detail.html?page=1&id=461|title=青梅市虎柏神社の祭礼行事 附祭礼行事関係記録 231点|website=東京都文化財情報データベース|accessdate=2020-7-22|ref={{sfnref|東京都文化財情報データベース 虎柏神社の祭礼行事}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[東京都の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ 東京の祭り]＞目次＞（青梅市）御殿入り祭 {{citation needed|とらかしわしんしや}} [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:Gion shrines]][[Category:Mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Torakashi Shrine|Torakashi Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft|draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |draft}} [[ja:虎柏神社]] {{afc comment|1=surprisingly notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 17:54, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|draft}} is a regional [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals - '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines === '''Enshrined Shrines''' - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines''' - '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine''' - '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access - '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References {{Reflist}} == Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|draft}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|draft}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|draft}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|draft}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals - '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival - '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival - '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines === '''Enshrined Shrines''' - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines''' - '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). - '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine''' - '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} - '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access - '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] - '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References {{Reflist}} == Related Articles -
