It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine) - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine) - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine) - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines === '''Enshrined Shrines''' - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines''' * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine''' * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) - ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' - ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) - ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References {{Reflist}} == Related Articles - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo (Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft|draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |draft}} [[ja:虎柏神社]] {{afc comment|1=surprisingly notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 17:54, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|draft}} is a regional [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == Related Articles== - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links== - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Torakashi Shrine]][[en:draft:Torakashi Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Torakashi Shrine|Torakashi Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft|draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |draft}} [[ja:虎柏神社]] {{afc comment|1=surprisingly notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 17:54, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|draft}} is a regional [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == Related Articles== - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links== - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Ōme, Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tokyo have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|draft}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|draft}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple|draft}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社|draft}} ==References== {{reflist|draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages with interlanguage linked {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Torakashi Shrine]][[en:draft:Torakashi Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Torakashi Shrine|Torakashi Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft|draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] |draft}} [[ja:虎柏神社]] {{afc comment|1=surprisingly notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 17:54, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|draft}} is a regional [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == Related Articles== - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links== - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival {{DEFAULTSORT:Torakashiwa Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Sōsha (Central Shrines)]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Ōme, Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Sites]] Here is a list of provincial soja including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Uenoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|draft}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|draft}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|draft}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|draft}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|draft}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|draft}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|draft}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|draft}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社|draft}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|draft}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|draft}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|draft}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple|draft}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社|draft}} ==References== {{reflist|draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Regional Sōja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Sōja It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha]] (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == During the [[Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Torakashi Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Torakashi Shrine|Torakashi Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=surprisingly notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 17:54, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|qid=Q11588867}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |draft| image= File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Q38276772|qid=Q38276772}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.802656|N|139.267125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[:en:Torii Gate|Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[:en:Minamoto no Tsunemoto|Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[:en:Asano Nagamasa|Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[:en:Chōfu|Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Ōme Station|Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[:en:Ōme Line|Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == Related Articles== - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links== - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]]'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[:en:Torii Gate|Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), [[:en:Minamoto no Tsunemoto|Minamoto no Tsunemoto]] enshrined deities from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), [[:en:Asano Nagamasa|Asano Nagamasa]] enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[:en:Chōfu|Chōfu]]. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Ōme Station|Higashi-Ōme Station]] on the [[:en:Ōme Line|Ōme Line]] * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == Related Articles== - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links== - Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in [[:en:Chōfu|Chōfu]].
