[[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |draft| image= File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Q38276772|WD=Q38276772}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.802656|N|139.267125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[:en:Torii Gate|Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), {{ill|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|en|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|WD=Q3099236|lt=Minamoto no Tsunemoto}} enshrined deities from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō}} era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), {{ill|Asano Nagamasa|en|Asano Nagamasa|WD=Q1067546|lt=Asano Nagamasa}} enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in {{ill|Chōfu|en|Chōfu|WD=Q210667|lt=Chōfu}}. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |draft| image= File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Q38276772|WD=Q38276772}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.802656|N|139.267125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[:en:Torii Gate|Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), {{ill|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|en|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|WD=Q3099236|lt=Minamoto no Tsunemoto}} enshrined deities from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō}} era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), {{ill|Asano Nagamasa|en|Asano Nagamasa|WD=Q1067546|lt=Asano Nagamasa}} enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in {{ill|Chōfu|en|Chōfu|WD=Q210667|lt=Chōfu}}. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Ōme Station|en|Higashi-Ōme Station|WD=Q844198|lt=Higashi-Ōme Station}} on the {{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ōme Line}} * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == Related Articles== - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links== - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |draft| image= File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Q38276772|WD=Q38276772|1=Nekabu}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.802656|N|139.267125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[:en:Torii Gate|Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), {{ill|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|en|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|WD=Q3099236|lt=Minamoto no Tsunemoto|1=Minamoto no Tsunemoto}} enshrined deities from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō|1=Eishō}} era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), {{ill|Asano Nagamasa|en|Asano Nagamasa|WD=Q1067546|lt=Asano Nagamasa|1=Asano Nagamasa}} enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in {{ill|Chōfu|en|Chōfu|WD=Q210667|lt=Chōfu|1=Chōfu}}. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Ōme Station|en|Higashi-Ōme Station|WD=Q844198|lt=Higashi-Ōme Station|1=Higashi-Ōme Station}} on the {{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ōme Line|1=Ōme Line}} * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |draft| image= File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Q38276772|WD=Q38276772|1=Nekabu}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.802656|N|139.267125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[:en:Torii Gate|Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), {{ill|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|en|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|WD=Q3099236|lt=Minamoto no Tsunemoto|1=Minamoto no Tsunemoto}} enshrined deities from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō|1=Eishō}} era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), {{ill|Asano Nagamasa|en|Asano Nagamasa|WD=Q1067546|lt=Asano Nagamasa|1=Asano Nagamasa}} enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in {{ill|Chōfu|en|Chōfu|WD=Q210667|lt=Chōfu|1=Chōfu}}. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Ōme Station|en|Higashi-Ōme Station|WD=Q844198|lt=Higashi-Ōme Station|1=Higashi-Ōme Station}} on the {{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ōme Line|1=Ōme Line}} * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == Related Articles== - [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] == External Links== - [http://members2.jcom.home.ne.jp/ichikondo/ Tokyo Festivals Index] > Ōme City > Otono-iri Festival Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |draft| image= File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Q38276772|WD=Q38276772|1=Nekabu}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.802656|N|139.267125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[:en:Torii Gate|Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), {{ill|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|en|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|WD=Q3099236|lt=Minamoto no Tsunemoto|1=Minamoto no Tsunemoto}} enshrined deities from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō|1=Eishō}} era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), {{ill|Asano Nagamasa|en|Asano Nagamasa|WD=Q1067546|lt=Asano Nagamasa|1=Asano Nagamasa}} enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in {{ill|Chōfu|en|Chōfu|WD=Q210667|lt=Chōfu|1=Chōfu}}. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Ōme Station|en|Higashi-Ōme Station|WD=Q844198|lt=Higashi-Ōme Station|1=Higashi-Ōme Station}} on the {{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ōme Line|1=Ōme Line}} * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|WD=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} * {{ill|Nanba In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in {{ill|Chōfu|en|Chōfu|WD=Q210667|lt=Chōfu|1=Chōfu}}. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site''' ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds'' (including two related historical documents){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Grounds}} * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' (Ritual Practices) ** ''Torakashiwa Shrine’s festivals'' (including 231 historical records){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Festivals}} == Access== * '''Rail''': 20-minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Ōme Station|en|Higashi-Ōme Station|WD=Q844198|lt=Higashi-Ōme Station|1=Higashi-Ōme Station}} on the {{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ōme Line|1=Ōme Line}} * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} == [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Torakashiwa-jinja]][[ja:虎柏神社]] [[ja:虎柏神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] |draft| image= File:Torakashiwa-jinja(Ohme) Haiden.jpg | prefecture= {{ill|Q38276772|WD=Q38276772|1=Nekabu}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.802656|N|139.267125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region. It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Torakashiwa Shrine | image = [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）拝殿.jpg|270px|]]
[[Haiden (Worship Hall)]] | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, [[Ōme City]], [[Negabu]] 1-316 | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]]
[[Kashikone no Kami]] | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (debated site) (Minor Shrine) • Former [[Gōsha]] | established = Unknown | honden = [[Three-bay structure|Sangen-sha]] [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]]{{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Database|Torakashiwa Shrine Honden}}
(Enclosed within an outer structure, not visible to the public) | annual_festival = August 26–28 | map = {{location map|Japan Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=9.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=16|lon_sec=1.6|label=Torakashiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|48|9.56|N|139|16|1.65|E}}}} }} '''Torakashiwa Shrine''' (虎柏神社, Torakashiwa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Negabu]], [[Ōme City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Formerly known as '''Suwa Shrine'''. This ancient shrine is listed among the eight shrines of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}'s [[Tama District]] in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. == Enshrined Deities== === Main Sanctuary (Torakashiwa Shrine)==== - [[Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami]] (Great Ancestral Harvest deity) - [[Kashikone no Kami]] === Eastern Hall (Suwa Shrine)==== - [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] - [[Yasakatome-no-Mikoto]] === Western Hall (Yakumo Shrine)==== - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] - [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]] == History== [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）鳥居.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[:en:Torii Gate|Torii Gate]]]] [[File:虎柏神社（青梅市）銘板.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Inscription Plaque]]]] The exact founding date of Torakashiwa Shrine is unknown, but it is said to have received land offerings during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is traditionally considered one of the shrines recorded in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]''. In 940 (Tengyō 3), {{ill|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|en|Minamoto no Tsunemoto|WD=Q3099236|lt=Minamoto no Tsunemoto|1=Minamoto no Tsunemoto}} enshrined deities from [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō|1=Eishō}} era (1504–1521) by [[Mita Ujimune]], lord of [[Katsunuma Castle]]. In 1588 (Tenshō 16), {{ill|Asano Nagamasa|en|Asano Nagamasa|WD=Q1067546|lt=Asano Nagamasa|1=Asano Nagamasa}} enshrined a plague god, believed to be [[Gozu Tennō]], forming what is now Yakumo Shrine. In 1590 (Tenshō 18), the shrine structure was formalized with the Suwa deities in the main hall, the Torakashiwa deity in the east, and the plague god in the west, becoming the ''Sōsha'' (central shrine) of [[Ozoki-gō]]. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was commonly known as ''Suwa Myōjin'' or ''Suwa-gū'', and even today, it is colloquially referred to as ''Osuwa-sama''. The nearest bus stop still bears the name "Suwa Shrine." In 1591 (Tenshō 19), the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] granted the shrine an estate of three ''koku'', which remained unchanged until the end of the Edo period. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine reverted to its original name, placing the Torakashiwa deity in the main sanctuary and relocating the Suwa deities to the eastern hall. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was officially Ranked as a [[Gōsha]]. The current ''Honden'' was reconstructed in 1734 (Kyōhō 19). It is a three-bay [[:en:Kirizuma-zukuri|Kirizuma-zukuri]] structure but is enclosed within an outer shrine hall, making it invisible to visitors. The shrine also contains auxiliary shrines, including Takamine Shrine and Inari Shrine, both originally established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto. There is an ongoing debate about the exact identity of Torakashiwa Shrine in the ''Engishiki''. Some scholars propose that ''Torakashiwa'' was mistakenly copied from ''Torakoma'' (虎狛), linking it to [[Kohaku Shrine]] in {{ill|Chōfu|en|Chōfu|WD=Q210667|lt=Chōfu|1=Chōfu}}. However, Torakashiwa Shrine itself promotes its identification as an ''Engishiki'' shrine through signage and historical claims. == festivals== * '''August 26''' - ''Otono-iri'' Festival * '''August 27''' - ''Wampan'' Ceremony & Takamine Shrine Festival * '''August 28''' - ''Otakiage'' Fire Ritual, Grand Festival, and Dedication Sumo Tournament == Auxiliary & Subsidiary Shrines==== === '''Enshrined Shrines'''==== - Yakumo Shrine - Suwa Shrine === '''Subsidiary Shrines'''==== * '''Fujiwara Shrine''' - Enshrines [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]]; established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto during the Tengyō era (938–947). * '''Inari Shrine''' - Originally within the western hall; established in the Tenshō era (1573–1593) by Fujiwara Uemon no Tayū; enshrines [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. === '''Off-site Shrine'''==== * '''Takamine Shrine''' - Established by Minamoto no Tsunemoto alongside the Suwa deities; enshrines [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]]. == Cultural Properties==== * '''Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property''' (Architecture) ** ''Honden'' (Main Hall) (including one ''Munafuda'' plaque){{sfn|Tokyo Cultural Property Station|1=Higashi-Ōme Station}} on the {{ill|Ōme Line|en|Ōme Line|WD=Q385398|lt=Ōme Line|1=Ōme Line}} * '''Bus''': Take the municipal bus to "Suwa Shrine" bus stop == References== {{Reflist}} ==
